---
title: "Import Data"
---


## Method 2 - `read.LIS()`

The second method, the `read.LIS()` function has a less automated approach on importing the data, and was more commonly used in the past. 

This method requires handling each country-year file separately by level. The imported datasets are then appended into a single data.frame, rather than being stored as a list.

Despite being superseded in most workflows, this function is still useful in specific cases—particularly for importing less frequently used files, such as those containing replicate weights in LWS.
 


```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
source("../src/read.LIS.R")
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# put here the code of read.LIS, then on a hidden file, such that is doesn't go to the github. 
source("../src/read.LIS.R") 
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}
data_h <- read.LIS(c("de18h", "es18h")) # Import h-level data for Lux. 2010 
data_p <- read.LIS(c("de18p", "es18p")) # Import p-level data for Lux. 2010 

full_data <- merge(data_p, data_h, by = c("dname", "hid"))  
class(full_data) 
```

<br>

#### Importing LWS data

To import LWS data, the syntax remains exactly the same, but again the <b>Project</b> must be changed in the drop-down menu.

![](/images/project_lws.png){fig-align="center"}


```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# put here the code of read.LIS, then on a hidden file, such that is doesn't go to the github. 
source("../src/read.LIS_for_lws.R") 
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}
varh <- c('hid', 'dname', 'own', 'dnw', 'inum')
varp <- c('hid', 'dname', 'inum', 'ppopwgt', 'age', 'sex', 'relation', 'emp', 'ptime1')


data_h <- read.LIS(c("de17h", "es17h"), vars = varh)
data_p <- read.LIS(c("de17p", "es17p"), vars = varp)

full_data <- merge(data_h, data_p, by = c("dname", "hid")) 
df <- merge(data_p , data_h , by = c("dname", "hid", "inum"))

names(df)
```
